text.title: Feedback Management Core Rules
text.summary: Pipeline turning scattered inbound feedback (support, NPS, app-store, sales, social) into ranked product themes via fixed taxonomy, segment-weighted scoring, and close-loop tracking.

rule.statement: All feedback channels (support, NPS, app-store, sales, social, in-app widget) MUST normalize into one canonical store with {source, date, user_id, segment, plan_tier, verbatim, classification} per item.
rule.rationale: Multi-store feedback gets re-summarized by each consumer; canonical store enables segment-weighted ranking and de-dup.

rule.statement: Classification MUST use a fixed published taxonomy (<=30 leaves, <=3 levels). New categories require a quarterly taxonomy-review and migration of past items.
rule.rationale: Drift in taxonomy breaks trend lines and makes recurring themes look like new ones.

rule.statement: Theme priority MUST factor segment_ARR × retention_risk × frequency. Raw vote counts alone are forbidden — they over-weight enterprise-customer power users.
rule.rationale: Raw counts privilege the loudest segment; ARR × retention reflects business impact.

rule.statement: Items flagged 'will-do' or 'under-consideration' MUST trigger a close-loop email to the requester within 14 days. Items flagged 'won't-do' MUST log a rationale.
rule.rationale: Without close-loop, requesters disengage and the feedback channel decays.

rule.statement: Once per month the PM MUST review the top 10 themes (segment-weighted) and link each to a backlog item, kill decision, or explicit deferral with rationale.
rule.rationale: Themes without monthly disposition pile up; the channel becomes a complaint mailbox, not a decision input.

rule.statement: If applicability conditions fail (any Skip-If trigger holds — pre-PMF with <5 items/week; compliance-bound feedback awaiting DPA review; sentiment-only loop without commitment to act; single-channel vendor-AI product), the agent MUST skip this methodology and route to the named alternative or to the methodology fallback.
rule.rationale: Mis-applying a methodology outside its preconditions degrades output quality and pollutes downstream artefacts; the explicit skip-rule keeps the routing tree honest.
